purpose: JSON Schema files defining the structured output contracts for every agent and synthesis pass in ultrainit. Each schema is paired with a `prompts/` file and consumed via `claude -p --json-schema` to coerce LLM output into typed, parseable JSON. Together they form the type system of the entire pipeline.

architecture.overview: 14 flat JSON Schema files, one per agent or synthesis pass. No subdirectories. All schemas use JSON Schema draft-07 conventions (object type, required arrays, additionalProperties: false) enforced universally. Schemas are stateless — they define output shape only, never execution logic. Three schemas use $defs for DRY reuse (notably commands.json). Two schemas (synthesis-docs.json, synthesis-tooling.json) store large markdown/bash blobs as plain strings, meaning schema validation cannot enforce internal syntax (YAML frontmatter, bash shebang, etc.) — that responsibility falls to Phase 5 validators.

subdirectories.purpose: All 14 schema files live flat in this directory, grouped conceptually by pipeline phase.

architecture.data_flow: Phase 1 gather agents each receive a schema via --json-schema and write output to .ultrainit/findings/<agent>.json. lib/agent.sh extracts .structured_output from the claude -p JSON envelope and writes the inner object. Phase 2 (ask) has no schema — developer answers are hand-written JSON. Phase 3 research agents use domain-research.json and mcp-recommendations.json. Phase 4 synthesis passes use synthesis-docs.json (Pass 1) and synthesis-tooling.json (Pass 2); their outputs are merged with jq -s '.[0] * .[1]' into synthesis/output.json, which conforms to synthesis-output.json. Phase 5 reads synthesis/output.json and dispatches to validate-skill.sh and validate-subagent.sh for artifact-level validation that schemas cannot express.

key_files.purpose: CRITICAL. Maps all project directories to role enums (23 values) and priority levels (high/medium/low). Output directly drives which directories get deep-dive module-analysis agents in Phase 1 Stage 2. Also controls which directories get subdirectory CLAUDE.md files.

CRITICAL. The richest schema — defines architecture, key_files (with importance enum), patterns, conventions, dependencies, gotchas, domain_terms, and skill_opportunities. The optional skill_opportunities array pre-identifies workflows for the synthesizer. One agent instance runs per high/medium priority directory.

Defines the output shape for Pass 2 synthesis: skills (name/description/content), hooks (filename/event enum/matcher/content), subagents, mcp_servers, and settings_hooks. Content fields store full markdown/bash as strings — schema cannot validate their internal syntax.

Pass 1 synthesis output: claude_md (full CLAUDE.md as single string) and subdirectory_claude_mds (array of {path, content}). Minimal schema — quality constraints like line count and generic-phrase detection are enforced externally by validate.sh.

Final merged schema combining Pass 1 and Pass 2 fields. Used by merge.sh as the authoritative shape when writing artifacts to .claude/. Only claude_md, skills, and hooks are required; all others optional.

Project identity — name, description, languages (with %), frameworks (with versions), monorepo flag, packages (optional), deployment target. Required by all downstream phases as source of tech stack context.

All developer commands categorized into build/test/lint/format/typecheck/other. Uses $defs/command_list for DRY reuse. Each command carries scope, ci_verified bool, and source path. Drives the Quick Reference table in CLAUDE.md.
Protected files list with path_pattern (glob), reason, and safe_alternative. Drives PreToolUse hook generation to block writes to .env, migration files, lock files, etc.

MCP server discovery output: name, package, description, relevance, and configuration (command/args/env). The env object is the only place in all 14 schemas where additionalProperties is NOT false — arbitrary env var keys are allowed.

Git history analysis: hotspots (top 20 files by change count), temporal coupling (top 10 co-changing file pairs), bug-fix density, ownership diffusion, recent activity by directory, commit patterns, branch naming conventions.

patterns.name: Universal strict mode
patterns.description: Every schema — all 14, at every nesting level — sets additionalProperties: false. This is the single most important convention. It causes claude -p to reject any fields the LLM hallucinated beyond the declared contract. Never omit this when adding a new schema or extending an existing one.

patterns.name: Enum-driven categorical fields
patterns.description: Categorical distinctions are always enums, never free-form strings. structure-scout.json has 23-value role enum and 3-value priority enum; patterns.json has a 9-value type enum; synthesis-tooling.json constrains hook events to [PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Notification, Stop]. This enables safe downstream filtering (e.g., select(.priority == "high")) without defensive string matching.

patterns.name: $defs for repeated item shapes
patterns.description: When the same object shape appears under multiple keys, extract it to $defs. commands.json defines command_list once and references it for all 6 categories (build, test, lint, format, typecheck, other). This is the only schema that uses $defs heavily; synthesis-tooling.json has similar repeated shapes (skill/hook/subagent) but does not consolidate them.

patterns.name: Markdown/bash content as opaque strings
patterns.description: synthesis-docs.json and synthesis-tooling.json store full document content (CLAUDE.md text, SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter, bash hook scripts) as plain JSON strings. Schema validation ends at the string boundary — structural validity of the content (valid YAML, shebang present, set -euo pipefail) must be checked externally by scripts/validate-skill.sh, scripts/validate-subagent.sh, and validate.sh hook checks.

patterns.name: Optional arrays for conditional data
patterns.description: Fields that only apply in certain contexts are excluded from the required array but declared as array types: identity.json.packages (only for monorepos), module-analysis.json.commands, module-analysis.json.skill_opportunities. All are arrays rather than nullable scalars, so consumers can always iterate safely with jq .field[]? without type-checking first.

patterns.name: Importance/priority enums for triage
patterns.description: Three schemas use tiered importance enums to drive downstream filtering decisions: structure-scout.json priority=[high,medium,low] determines which directories spawn deep-dive agents; module-analysis.json key_files importance=[critical,important,reference] drives how files are presented in CLAUDE.md; git-forensics.json uses implicit rank ordering (top-N arrays). Always use enum tiers rather than numeric scores.

patterns.name: Evidence-paired findings
patterns.description: Schemas that describe code patterns require evidence_files (array of paths) alongside the pattern description. patterns.json.patterns[].evidence_files and module-analysis.json.key_files[].path enforce that LLM claims are grounded in real file references, not invented. This also enables validate-skill.sh to check that skill content references ≥3 real paths.

conventions.naming: Schema filenames match agent names exactly (identity.json ↔ identity agent, structure-scout.json ↔ structure-scout agent) except for three synthesis schemas (synthesis-docs.json, synthesis-tooling.json, synthesis-output.json) which map to synthesis passes, not agents. Module deep-dive outputs are named module-<dirname>.json at runtime, all sharing the same module-analysis.json schema.
conventions.imports: Schemas are referenced by absolute path assembled at runtime in lib/gather.sh and lib/synthesize.sh via $SCRIPT_DIR/schemas/<name>.json. No bundling or $schema URI declarations. JSON Schema draft version is implicit (draft-07 conventions used but not declared).
conventions.error_handling: Schema violations surface as claude -p exit code != 0 with error text on stdout (not stderr). lib/agent.sh captures stdout on failure and appends to the agent's .stderr log for diagnose_phase_failure. A secondary jq type check (type == "object" or "array") after extraction catches cases where claude returns a valid JSON string rather than an object.
other: All boolean fields use JSON boolean (true/false), never strings ('yes'/'no') or integers.
All array fields are always arrays — never null or a single object — even when empty. This lets consumers use jq .field[] without defensive checks.
The env object in mcp-recommendations.json and synthesis-tooling.json MCP server entries is the only intentional additionalProperties: true — arbitrary env var key names are required.
Description fields on schema properties are prescriptive LLM instructions (e.g., 'Detailed description of the pattern including HOW to follow it'), not documentation for humans.
No $schema, $id, or version fields in any schema file. Versioning is implicit via git history.

internal: lib/agent.sh — consumes all gather schemas via --json-schema flag; extracts .structured_output from envelope
lib/gather.sh — references schemas/identity.json, commands.json, git-forensics.json, patterns.json, tooling.json, docs.json, security.json, structure-scout.json, module-analysis.json
lib/synthesize.sh — references schemas/synthesis-docs.json, synthesis-tooling.json, synthesis-output.json; merges pass outputs
lib/validate.sh — reads synthesis/output.json (synthesis-output.json shape); dispatches to scripts/validate-skill.sh and scripts/validate-subagent.sh
lib/merge.sh — reads synthesis/output.json to write .claude/ artifacts

external_key.name: claude CLI (--json-schema flag)
external_key.purpose: Structured output enforcement — passes schema to the model to coerce output shape

external_key.name: jq
external_key.purpose: All JSON extraction, merging (jq -s '.[0] * .[1]'), and validation (type checks) after agent output is written to findings files

domain_terms.meaning: The field name inside claude -p's JSON envelope that contains the schema-conformant object. lib/agent.sh extracts this with jq '.structured_output // .result // .'
The inner structured object written to .ultrainit/findings/<agent>.json after extraction from the claude envelope
A module-analysis agent instance spawned per directory; all share module-analysis.json schema but write to distinct finding files (module-<dirname>.json)
One of two large-context claude -p calls in Phase 4; Pass 1 uses synthesis-docs.json, Pass 2 uses synthesis-tooling.json
Optional array in module-analysis.json where the analysis agent pre-identifies workflows worth turning into Claude Code skills; used as hints by the synthesizer
Array of real file paths that prove a pattern exists; required in patterns.json and module-analysis.json to prevent hallucinated claims
Wiring entries in synthesis-tooling.json that map hook scripts to .claude/settings.json event registrations; must have 1:1 correspondence with hooks[] entries
Optional field on hooks and settings_hooks that scopes a Pre/PostToolUse hook to a specific tool name (e.g., 'Write', 'Edit')

gotchas.issue: Enum constraints in schemas are guidance, not hard enforcement. claude -p may return values outside declared enums (e.g., priority: 'critical' instead of 'high'). Downstream jq filters using select(.priority == 'high') silently skip unexpected values rather than erroring.
gotchas.solution: Add explicit jq validation in lib/gather.sh after structure-scout output is written: check that all priority values are in [high, medium, low] before spawning deep-dive agents. Alternatively, treat unknown priorities as 'medium'.

gotchas.issue: Angle brackets in skill descriptions break YAML frontmatter parsing. A description like 'Use for <adding endpoints>' causes YAML parse errors when Claude Code reads the skill file.
gotchas.solution: lib/synthesize.sh:postprocess_descriptions strips angle brackets after extraction. The synthesizer-tooling.md prompt also explicitly forbids them. When writing new synthesis prompts, repeat this constraint.

gotchas.issue: identity.json.packages has a conditional dependency on monorepo: true, but the schema cannot express this — packages is simply absent from the required array. Agents analyzing non-monorepos may still emit packages if confused.
gotchas.solution: Downstream consumers must check monorepo === true before iterating packages. Never assume packages is absent just because the schema doesn't require it.

gotchas.issue: synthesis-output.json is not actually passed to claude -p as a --json-schema. It is only the expected shape of the jq-merged file (jq -s '.[0] * .[1]' output-docs.json output-tooling.json). If either pass produces an unexpected top-level key, the merge silently wins with the second file's value.
gotchas.solution: After merging, validate the merged output against synthesis-output.json manually (e.g., via ajv or a jq type check) before passing to merge.sh.

gotchas.issue: The mcp-recommendations.json schema and the mcp_servers field in synthesis-tooling.json have slightly different shapes. mcp-recommendations includes relevance and a nested configuration object; synthesis-tooling.json flattens command/args/env to the top level. Copying a mcp-recommendations finding directly into synthesis output will fail.
gotchas.solution: The synthesizer prompt (synthesizer-tooling.md) must reshape MCP data. When debugging MCP config issues, check both schemas side-by-side.

gotchas.issue: developer-answers.json (Phase 2 output) has no schema. It is hand-assembled by lib/ask.sh and consumed verbatim in synthesis context. If ask.sh changes field names, synthesize.sh jq expressions silently return null.
gotchas.solution: Treat developer-answers.json field names (project_purpose, deployment_target, never_do, common_mistakes, additional_context) as a de-facto schema. Any change to ask.sh output keys requires updating all jq expressions in synthesize.sh.

gotchas.issue: settings_hooks[] entries must have a matching entry in hooks[]. The schema does not enforce this relationship — a synthesizer could generate settings_hooks without corresponding hook scripts. validate.sh checks this, but only after writing.
gotchas.solution: If adding hooks to a synthesizer prompt, always generate both the hooks[] entry (with content) and the settings_hooks[] wiring entry in the same response. Validate with: for each settings_hooks[].command basename, a matching hooks[].filename must exist.

gotchas.issue: module-analysis.json is reused for all deep-dive agents but output files are named module-<dirname>.json. If two directories have the same basename (e.g., src/api and lib/api), their findings files collide.
gotchas.solution: lib/gather.sh should use a sanitized full-path slug (e.g., src-api, lib-api) rather than just the basename when naming module findings files.

skill_opportunities.name: add-agent-schema
skill_opportunities.description: Workflow for adding a new agent + schema pair: create the JSON schema, write the matching prompt in prompts/, register the agent call in lib/gather.sh or lib/research.sh, add a fixture in tests/fixtures/findings/, and wire a mock dispatch entry.
workflow_steps: Create schemas/<agent-name>.json with required fields, additionalProperties: false at all levels, and appropriate enums
Create prompts/<agent-name>.md with system prompt and output contract examples
Add run_agent call in lib/gather.sh (Stage 1 or Stage 2) with correct model and allowedTools
Add a realistic fixture JSON in tests/fixtures/findings/<agent-name>.json
Add dispatch entry in tests/helpers/mock_claude.bash for the new agent name
Run make test-unit to verify agent_run.bats and gather integration tests pass

skill_opportunities.name: validate-schema-output
skill_opportunities.description: Given a findings JSON file, validate it against its corresponding schema using ajv or jq structural checks. Useful after agent failures or when debugging unexpected synthesis behavior.
workflow_steps: Identify the agent name from the findings filename (e.g., identity.json → schemas/identity.json)
Report any additionalProperties violations or missing required fields
If no ajv available, use jq to spot-check required fields and enum values

commands.purpose: Inspect the most complex schema (23-value role enum, priority levels, directory mapping)
List all top-level output fields for Pass 2 synthesis
List all valid directory role values
Verify all schemas enforce strict mode (should equal total schema count)
